findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "epss_timeseries" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

description:
Run a public ZEN SecDB feed report.

## What this tool does
Executes a predefined report on ZEN SecDB public feed data and returns structured results for analytics, trends, distributions, and top-N summaries.

Supported reports can cover public datasets such as:
- CVEs
- security advisories
- EPSS
-

labels.name: purl_audit
labels.description:
Perform a software package vulnerability audit using SecDB.

## What this tool does
Analyzes a list of software packages identified by PURL (Package URL) and returns
vulnerability information plus a Markdown summary. The audit results are based
exclusively on the package list provided.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: vulnerability_score, feed_report_catalog, feed_report, purl_audit · Private data: sightings_search, vulnerability_info, purl_audit · Public sink: purl_audit · Destructive: epss_timeseries, vulnerability_info, feed_report.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: vulnerability_score, feed_report_catalog, feed_report, purl_audit · Private data: sightings_search, vulnerability_info, purl_audit · Public sink: purl_audit · Destructive: epss_timeseries, vulnerability_info, feed_report

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
